theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Technology (+2.72%) and Industrials (+0.83%) led the market today, while Consumer Staples (-1.03%) and Communication Svcs (-0.45%) brought up the rear. Performance didn't cluster cleanly by style — growth averaged +0.81%, defensives -0.41%, and value/cyclicals +0.45% — more a stock-by-stock market than a single broad theme. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.92%, Dow +0.98%, Nasdaq +1.38% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps bucked the broader trend, with the Russell 2000 up +1.26%, outperforming large caps. The VIX fell 8.26% to 17.11, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 0.63% to $4,076.50, crude oil was little changed at $84.48, and bitcoin gained 1.99% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
